abhijan: to be born to, lineage (abhi- + √jan 'be born')
adhyātma: pertaining to the self; the inward principle
adṛṣṭa: unseen (a- + dṛṣṭa past-pple. 'seen', from √dṛś); the unmanifest karmic residue
akṣara: imperishable (a- + kṣara, from √kṣar 'flow/perish'); syllable; the Imperishable (Brahman)
amṛta: immortal, the deathless, nectar (a- + mṛta past-pple. 'died', from √mṛ)
ananta: endless, infinite (an- + anta 'end'); an epithet of Viṣṇu
arjuna: Arjuna (the third Pāṇḍava); also: white, bright
asatkṛ: to dishonor (asat + √kṛ: 'to make untrue / treat as nothing')
aś: to eat / to pervade (verbal root, two homophonous roots)
aśocya: not to be grieved over (a- + śocya gerundive of √śuc)
aśraddadhāna: without faith (a- + śraddadhāna pres.-pple. of √śrad-dhā 'have faith')
bhaj: to share, partake of, worship, love (verbal root)
bharata: Bharata (ancestor of the Kurus); a descendant of Bharata
bhārata: descendant of Bharata; epithet of Arjuna
bhū: to be, become; the earth (verbal root / noun)
brahman: Brahman (the Absolute); also: the Veda; sacred utterance
dakṣiṇāyana: the southern course of the sun (winter solstice direction)
dehin: embodied being; the soul in a body
dhārtarāṣṭra: son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (esp. Duryodhana et al.)
ga: going, going to (suffix); short for gam
guṇa: quality, attribute (esp. the three guṇas: sattva/rajas/tamas)
iha: here, in this world, in this life
jan: to be born; to produce (verbal root)
karman: action, deed, the law of action
kāmyā: desirable; performed for desire of result
kūṭastha: fixed at the summit, immutable (kūṭa 'peak' + stha 'standing')
kṣetra: field, body (as the field of experience)
lip: to smear, anoint, taint, stain (verbal root)
mad: I, me (1st person pronoun stem); also: to rejoice (verbal root)
manas: mind (lower mind), the inner organ of perception
mā: do not (prohibitive); also: to measure (verbal root)
prakṛti: primordial nature (pra- + √kṛ 'do' — 'that from which all is made')
puruṣa: person, man; the cosmic Person; the Self (Sāṅkhya/Vedānta)
pārtha: son of Pṛthā (Kuntī); epithet of Arjuna
pāṇḍava: son of Pāṇḍu (the five Pāṇḍava brothers)
sambhāvay: to cause to be, regard, honor
sattva: purity, lightness (the first guṇa); being, essence
saṅga: attachment, contact (= saṅga in earlier dict — alternate spelling)
stambh: to support, hold up, paralyze (verbal root)
sāttvika: sāttvika (derived from sattva: 'pertaining to the sattva guṇa')
tap: to heat, perform tapas / austerity (verbal root)
tyaj: to abandon, give up, renounce (verbal root)
tā: (feminine suffix; or stem-form of various words — context-dependent)
tāmasa: tāmasa (derived from tamas 'darkness': 'pertaining to the tamas guṇa')
vasu: Vasu (a class of eight deities); wealth, treasure
vyavasāya: resolve, determination, settled intention (vi- + ava- + √so 'destroy/finish' — 'cutting through to a decision')
vṛt: to be, exist, occur, turn (verbal root)
yam: to restrain, control (verbal root); also: which (relative)
yudhiṣṭhira: Yudhiṣṭhira (the eldest Pāṇḍava, son of Dharma)
yuj: to yoke, join, engage in (verbal root)
āditya: Āditya, son of Aditi; the sun
ātman: the Self, soul; one's own self
āśri: to take refuge in, resort to
śākhā: branch (of a tree); recension of the Veda
indriya: sense, sense-organ; the eleven indriyas (5 jñānendriyas + 5 karmendriyas + manas)
deva: god, deity, celestial being; the devas
dharma: duty, law, righteousness; the principle of right action; one's own nature (sva-dharma)
rūpa: form, shape, appearance; the visible aspect
tva: you-ness; abstract suffix making 'X-ness' from X
ādi: beginning, origin; first; (suffix: 'X and so on')
rājasa: rājasic; pertaining to the rajas guṇa (passion, activity)
yat: who, which (relative pronoun, variant of yad); also: to strive, exert (verbal root)
prāṇa: vital breath, life-energy; the prāṇas (vital airs)
śreyas: better, superior; the higher good (vs preyas, the pleasant)
viś: to enter (verbal root); also: settler, person (Vaiśya class)
bhīṣma: Bhīṣma (the Kuru patriarch); also: terrible, frightful
maya: consisting of (suffix); also: Maya the asura architect
prapad: to take refuge in, surrender to (pra- + √pad)
āsthā: to take a stand on, resort to (ā- + √sthā)
sāṃkhya: Sāṃkhya (one of the six darśanas); knowledge, enumeration (from √khyā)
kṛt: doer, maker (from √kṛ); the kṛt-suffixes in Pāṇini
hṛṣīkeśa: lord of the senses (epithet of Kṛṣṇa: hṛṣīka 'sense' + īśa 'lord')
siddha: perfected, accomplished; a class of celestial beings
hā: to abandon, give up (verbal root); also: alas (interjection)
ram: to delight in, sport (verbal root); rāma
mānasa: pertaining to the mind; mental (from manas)
sat: being, true, real; the existent (present participle of √as)
śrī: Śrī, the goddess; auspicious prefix; glory, beauty, fortune
janārdana: rouser of people (epithet of Kṛṣṇa: jana + √ard)
bhavat: you (respectful 2nd-person pronoun); also: present participle of √bhū
apāna: downward breath (one of the five prāṇas)
āgama: tradition, scripture, that which has come down (ā- + √gam)
madhusūdana: slayer of the demon Madhu (epithet of Kṛṣṇa)
darśay: to show, cause to see (causative of √dṛś)
bhāvay: to bring into being, contemplate (causative of √bhū)
darśana: seeing, vision; one of the six philosophical schools
saṃsāra: the wheel of conditioned existence (sam- + √sṛ 'flow together')
droṇa: Droṇa (the brahmin warrior-teacher of the Pāṇḍavas + Kauravas)
udāsīna: indifferent, neutral; one who sits apart (ud- + ā- + √sad)
saṃjan: to arise together, be born of (sam- + √jan)
karaṇa: instrument, organ, means; act of doing
bhagavant: the Blessed One; possessing bhaga (the six perfections); the Bhagavān
sampradāya: tradition handed down, lineage transmission (sam- + pra- + √dā)
karṇa: Karṇa (the half-brother of the Pāṇḍavas); also: ear
śikhaṇḍin: Śikhaṇḍin (the warrior whose presence let Bhīṣma fall)
sūta: charioteer, bard (Sañjaya is sūta to Dhṛtarāṣṭra)
sātyaki: Sātyaki (Yādava warrior on the Pāṇḍava side)
virāṭa: Virāṭa (king who sheltered the Pāṇḍavas in exile)
drupada: Drupada (king of Pāñcāla, father of Draupadī)
ājamīḍha: descendant of King Ajamīḍha (epithet of Yudhiṣṭhira)
devadatta: Devadatta (Arjuna's conch); also: 'God-given' (a generic name like 'John Doe')
tūrya: musical instrument; the four kinds of instruments
kapi-dhvaja: monkey-bannered (epithet of Arjuna — Hanuman on his banner)
yotsya: those who will fight (future participle of √yudh)
anīkayos: of the two armies (genitive dual of anīka)
senayos: of the two armies (genitive dual of senā)
viśvarūpa: having all forms; cosmic-form (epithet of Kṛṣṇa in Ch 11)
abhakta: (a- + bhakta: devotee; one who is devoted)
yogeśvara: lord of yoga (yoga + īśvara); epithet of Kṛṣṇa
saṃjaya: Saṃjaya (the charioteer-narrator of the Gītā); also: victory
carācara: moving and unmoving (cara + acara); all creation
upāśri: to take refuge in, resort to (upa- + ā- + √śri)
pitāmaha: grandfather (pitṛ + mahat); epithet of Bhīṣma; also of Brahmā
guḍākeśa: Guḍākeśa (epithet of Arjuna, 'thick-haired' or 'conqueror of sleep')
pravyathay: to cause to tremble, agitate (pra- + caus. of √vyath)
vivarjay: to abandon, give up (caus. of vi- + √vṛj)
diś: direction, quarter; to point out (verbal root)
bhagavat: the Blessed One, Lord (possessor of bhaga 'fortune')
vyāsa: Vyāsa (compiler of the Mahābhārata and Vedas)
garīyas: heavier, weightier, more venerable (compar. of guru)
acyuta: the unfallen, imperishable (a- + cyuta, from √cyu); epithet of Kṛṣṇa
āveśay: to cause to enter, fix upon (caus. of ā- + √viś)
ekastha: standing as one, situated together (eka + stha)
mata: thought, opinion; held to be (past-pple. of √man)
mahī: the earth, great one (fem. of mahat)
aśvattha: the sacred fig tree (Ficus religiosa); the world-tree
sākṣin: witness (sa + akṣi 'eye'; one who sees with own eyes)
bhāvana: causing to be, meditation, contemplation (caus. of √bhū)
kṛtāñjali: with palms joined in salutation (kṛta + añjali)
samupasthā: to stand near, present oneself (sam- + upa- + √sthā)
varuṇa: Varuṇa (god of waters and cosmic order)
yuktatama: most yoked, most disciplined (superl. of yukta)
pratiṣṭhāpay: to establish, set up (caus. of prati- + √sthā)
pat: to fall, fly (verbal root); also: lord, master
arpay: to offer, deliver, place upon (caus. of √ṛ)
nirdvaṃdva: free from pairs of opposites (nis- + dvaṃdva)
namasy: to pay homage to (denom. of namas)
maheśvara: Great Lord (mahā + īśvara); epithet of Śiva
kasmāt: from what cause? why? (abl. of ka)
samantāt: all around, on every side (abl. of samanta)
dhṛtarāṣṭra: Dhṛtarāṣṭra (the blind Kuru king, father of the Kauravas)
niveśay: to cause to settle, fix in (caus. of ni- + √viś)
sādhya: to be accomplished; a class of celestial beings
ūṣmapa: drinking warmth/vapor; a class of ancestors (ūṣma + pa)
ekākṣara: the single syllable (eka + akṣara); i.e., Oṃ
gītā: the Song; the Bhagavad-Gītā (past-pple. fem. of √gai)
śri: to resort to, take refuge (verbal root)
mahīkṣit: ruler of the earth, king (mahī + kṣit)
vyapāśri: to take refuge in, depend on (vi- + apa- + √śri)
h: (rare lemma fragment; meaning unclear — see commentaries)
romaharṣa: horripilation, hair standing on end (roman + harṣa)
uttarāyaṇa: the northern course (of the sun); winter solstice
pāṇḍu: Pāṇḍu (father of the Pāṇḍavas); also: pale
samakṣa: before the eyes, in presence of (sam + akṣi)
dānava: Dānava (a class of demons, sons of Danu)
vas: to dwell; to wear; to shine (verbal root)
āstikya: belief in the existence (of God/scripture); piety (from āstika)
yauvana: youth, the prime of life (from yuvan)
vyatitṛ: to cross over, pass beyond (vi- + ati- + √tṛ)
nirveda: indifference, disgust with the world (nis- + veda)
uttara: upper, northern, later, superior (compar. of ud)
piṇḍa: lump, ball; offering of rice to ancestors
yuyudhāna: Yuyudhāna (a Pāṇḍava ally; also called Sātyaki)
samprakīrtay: to proclaim fully (caus. of sam- + pra- + √kīrt)
aryaman: Aryaman (a solar deity; chief of the manes)
bhaviṣya: future, what is to be (fut. of √bhū)
vidāray: to tear apart, split (caus. of vi- + √dṝ)
vyanunāday: to make resound (vi- + anu- + caus. of √nad)
kṛte: for the sake of, on account of (loc. of kṛta)
aśvatthāman: Aśvatthāman (son of Droṇa, a Kaurava warrior)
vyape: to drive away, ward off (vi- + apa- + √i)
kṣetrin: field-owner; the soul as knower of the kṣetra (kṣetra + -in)
samupāśri: to take refuge in (sam- + upa- + ā- + √śri)
sthāṇu: stationary, fixed; tree-trunk; epithet of Śiva
akṛtātman: one whose self is unformed/uncontrolled (a- + kṛta + ātman)
pātaka: sin, that which causes to fall (from √pat)
dhṛṣṭaketu: Dhṛṣṭaketu (a Pāṇḍava ally, king of the Cedis)
apekṣ: to look toward, regard, depend on (apa- + √īkṣ)
nistraiguṇya: free from the three guṇas (nis- + traiguṇya)
traividya: knower of the three Vedas (from trividyā)
sma: indeed (emphatic particle, often with present = past tense)
dhṛṣṭadyumna: Dhṛṣṭadyumna (Pāñcāla prince, commander of the Pāṇḍava army)
jāhnavī: Jāhnavī (the river Ganges, daughter of Jahnu)
praṇipāta: prostration, falling at the feet (pra- + ni- + √pat)
avanipāla: king, protector of the earth (avani + pāla)
daitya: Daitya (a class of demons, sons of Diti)
kalay: to impel, count, perceive (denom. from kalā)
avadhya: not to be slain (a- + vadhya, from √vadh)
ayathāvat: not as it should be, improperly (a- + yathāvat)
śārīra: bodily, pertaining to the body (from śarīra)
mahābhūta: great element (mahā + bhūta); the five gross elements
avasāday: to bring down, depress (caus. of ava- + √sad)
dvijottama: best of the twice-born, brāhmaṇa (dvija + uttama)
adhidaivata: the divine plane, presiding deity (adhi- + daivata)
soma: Soma (the ritual plant/drink and its deity); the moon
marīci: Marīci (a primal sage; ray of light)
anurañj: to be devoted to, attach to (anu- + √rañj)
34: (numeric token; not a lemma — likely OCR/parse artifact)
āpṛthivī: as far as the earth (ā + pṛthivī)
pravartay: to set in motion, propel (caus. of pra- + √vṛt)
vaiśvānara: the universal fire, digestive fire ('belonging to all men')
yādava: Yādava (descendant of Yadu); epithet of Kṛṣṇa
bhrāmay: to whirl, cause to wander (caus. of √bhram)
pratyavāya: adverse result, sin of omission (prati- + ava- + √i)
paurvadehika: belonging to a former body (from pūrva + deha)
rathopastha: the seat of the chariot (ratha + upastha)
manoratha: wish, desire ('chariot of the mind', manas + ratha)
cakrin: wielder of the discus (cakra + -in); epithet of Viṣṇu
varṇya: to be classed, of a varṇa (from varṇa)
vedānta: the end of the Veda; Upaniṣadic teaching (veda + anta)
saṃpravṛt: to come about, set forth (sam- + pra- + √vṛt)
hvī: (rare lemma; meaning unclear — possibly variant of √hve 'call')
avācya: unspeakable, not to be said (a- + vācya)
